steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The researchers concluded that the tectonic plate regime began 3.2 billion years ago. The defining feature of that regime is that it allows lithospheric and mantle material to mix. Supporting evidence must show that mixing started at exactly that point — lithospheric material appearing in mantle-derived rocks only after 3.2 billion years ago.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Mantle-derived rocks older than 3.2 billion years show significantly more compositional diversity than lithospheric rocks older than 3.2 billion years do.': Compares diversity between rock types before the transition; says nothing about mixing beginning at 3.2 billion years. ✗
  • Choice B — 'There is a positive correlation between the age of lithospheric rocks and their chemical similarity to mantle-derived rocks, and that correlation increases significantly in strength at around 3.2 billion years old.': Describes a correlation within lithospheric rocks, not evidence of mantle material crossing into lithospheric rock or vice versa at the transition point. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Mantle-derived rocks younger than 3.2 billion years contain some material that is not found in older mantle-derived rocks but is found in older and contemporaneous lithospheric rocks.': Lithospheric material appears in younger mantle-derived rocks but not older ones — directly showing mixing began at 3.2 billion years ago. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Among rocks known to be older than 3.2 billion years, significantly more are mantle derived than lithospheric, but the opposite is true for the rocks younger than 3.2 billion years.': Addresses population ratios, not chemical mixing between rock types. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
